propositions: The stale index.lock reclaim reads the lock's mtime and later removes the same pathname without re-confirming it is the same file, creating a TOCTOU window.
A peer can replace a stale index.lock with a fresh live lock between the stat and the unlink, causing rebar to unlink the peer's new live lock.
The reclaim path should re-validate file identity (device and inode) and age immediately before removal, aborting the unlink on mismatch.
No existing test replaces the inode during reclamation; a deterministic regression test should pause reclaim after statting, swap in a fresh file, and assert the replacement survives.
The fix must not change the stale threshold constant or the retry loop's public behavior, and existing stationary-stale and young-lock tests must still pass.
